meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn a professional client workflow to generate high-value project ideas using Antigravity and Claude Code. Start building today.
content.answer_block: The secret to project paralysis is a lack of real-world friction. By simulating a client workflow, you identify actual pain points that demand software solutions. Instead of building generic clones, you will use Antigravity to architect systems and Claude Code to execute them, turning abstract requirements into high-value portfolio pieces.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat, mocking your lack of direction. You have the technical skills, but the 'what' remains a mystery. Most tutorials teach you how to write code, but they fail to teach you how to find problems worth solving. This is the chasm between a student and a software engineer.

Today, we close that gap. We are shifting your focus from 'coding exercises' to 'client-driven engineering.' By adopting a professional client workflow, you will extract requirements from hypothetical business scenarios, transforming vague needs into production-grade applications that actually matter.

steps.title: Define the Client Persona
steps.description: Establish the business context. Without a client, there is no scope. Choose a niche like local retail, logistics, or freelance management to define your project boundaries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software consultant. Generate 3 distinct business scenarios for a local service-based business that is currently struggling with manual data entry. Provide a brief overview of their workflow, pain points, and desired outcome for a SaaS MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project requirements document (PRD) based on the chosen business scenario from Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, map the data. A robust schema prevents technical debt and ensures your app scales beyond a simple demo.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the PRD for the [Project Name], design a normalized PostgreSQL schema. Include entity-relationship explanations, primary keys, and foreign key constraints for the core features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a migrations folder and generate SQL files for the defined schema.'

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Establish how your frontend communicates with your backend. This enforces discipline and prevents feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define the REST API endpoints for the [Project Name]. Include request/response bodies, HTTP methods, and status codes for the core CRUD operations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the defined API endpoints using Express.js and validate them with Zod schemas.'

steps.title: Build the Core Module
steps.description: Focus on the 'killer feature'—the one part of the app that provides the most value to your simulated client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the business logic for the primary module of [Project Name]. Ensure it handles edge cases like empty inputs and data validation errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core business logic module and add unit tests using Jest.'

steps.title: Construct the UI Prototype
steps.description: Translate your logic into a functional interface. Focus on usability and clarity rather than aesthetic perfection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a component structure for the frontend of [Project Name] using React and Tailwind CSS. Focus on a clean, professional dashboard layout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the main dashboard component and connect it to the backend API endpoints.'

pro_tips: Always build for a 'user persona' rather than 'yourself' to avoid building features nobody needs.
Use Claude Code to document every decision in a README.md file; it makes your portfolio look 10x more professional.
Focus on the 'Happy Path' first, then use Antigravity to generate edge-case error handling.
Treat your Git commit messages like professional documentation for a team lead to review.
Limit your scope to a 48-hour build cycle to prevent 'tutorial hell' and ensure project completion.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is functional, perform a 'Code Review' using Claude Code to identify potential refactoring opportunities, then deploy your project to Vercel or Railway.
content.conversion_block: You now have the workflow, the tools, and the mindset to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every great developer started exactly where you are, but the ones who succeed are the ones who build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
